title: Amadeus test API 500s — characterise the failure pattern, reproduce with curl, fix or file
description: The Amadeus test API has been returning HTTP 500 on flights/locations endpoints persistently since the multi-provider round (May 9). The travel agent's friendly-error widget masks the upstream failure — graceful degradation works — but we don't actually know whether it's our payload, Amadeus sandbox flake, an auth quirk, or a regional issue. The audit side-effect round (item #4) gives us a concrete dataset: every render_amadeus_failure row in travel_agent_events has the originating intent, the envelope_status_code, and the failure envelope. This round uses that data to characterise the pattern, reproduces a representative failure with a direct curl using the exact payload our MCP playbook sends, compares against Amadeus self-service docs, and either ships a small fix in mcp/amadeus.yaml OR confirms it's sandbox flake and documents next steps. Cap: 1 ops PR (only if there's a fix on our side) + 1 sync-issue memory entry.

design_brief_for_codex.current_state: render_amadeus_failure has been the dominant tail step in recent travel smokes (Phase 3, hotels/activities, refinement-form, audit, Phase 4 all noted Amadeus 500s). Our friendly-error widget still surfaces, so users see a useful response. But we have zero diagnosis of why the 500s happen. The audit table now captures every failure with payload.envelope_status_code and payload.classified_intent — that's the dataset to use.
design_brief_for_codex.endpoints_to_check:
The MCP playbook (`automation/agents/mcp/amadeus.yaml`) makes urllib calls to:
  - search_flights → POST `https://test.api.amadeus.com/v2/shopping/flight-offers`
  - search_hotels → GET `https://test.api.amadeus.com/v1/reference-data/locations/hotels/by-city?cityCode=...`
  - search_locations → GET `https://test.api.amadeus.com/v1/reference-data/locations?keyword=...`
  - search_activities → GET `https://test.api.amadeus.com/v1/shopping/activities?latitude=...&longitude=...&radius=...`
  - get_token → standard OAuth2 client_credentials
The failure pattern may be specific to one or more of these endpoints, or environment-wide.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_1_characterise:
SQL the audit table for the failure pattern:
```sql
SELECT
  payload->>'render_kind' AS intent,
  (payload->>'envelope_status_code')::int AS status_code,
  COUNT(*) AS occurrences,
  MIN(event_time) AS first_seen,
  MAX(event_time) AS last_seen
FROM travel_agent_events
WHERE event_type = 'render_amadeus_failure'
  AND event_time > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY occurrences DESC;
```
Answer: which intent(s) hit 500, how often, time-of-day clustering. Also pull a couple of full failure payloads via:
```sql
SELECT execution_id, event_time, payload
FROM travel_agent_events
WHERE event_type = 'render_amadeus_failure' AND payload->>'envelope_status_code' = '500'
ORDER BY event_time DESC LIMIT 5;
```
Look for any error body the MCP playbook captured (envelope.data.error or envelope.text).
design_brief_for_codex.phase_2_reproduce_with_curl:
Pick the most-failing endpoint from phase 1. Build a curl using:
  - The Amadeus token: `curl -X POST https://test.api.amadeus.com/v1/security/oauth2/token -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' -d "grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=$ID&client_secret=$SECRET"` then `jq -r .access_token`.
  - The endpoint with our exact payload (e.g. `POST /v2/shopping/flight-offers` with the exact body we send: originLocationCode, destinationLocationCode, departureDate, adults, currencyCode, max).
Verify the 500 reproduces and capture the full response body — Amadeus typically includes a JSON `errors[]` array with `status`, `code`, `title`, `detail`. That's the diagnostic gold.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_3_compare_against_docs:
Fetch the relevant Amadeus self-service test API spec (`https://developers.amadeus.com/self-service/category/flights/api-doc/flight-offers-search` or the equivalent for the failing endpoint). Compare:
  - Required vs optional parameters — are we missing one?
  - Parameter formats — IATA codes uppercase, dates YYYY-MM-DD, currencyCode ISO 4217, adults 1-9?
  - Path/version — are we calling /v2/shopping/flight-offers or /v1/...? Some endpoints have moved.
  - Headers — are we sending the right Accept/Content-Type?
  - Test API quirks — Amadeus test API is known to have less data than production; some valid queries return 500 because the test sandbox has no matching data even when production would.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_4_verdict_and_optional_fix:
Three possible verdicts:

  (a) **Our payload has a fixable bug.** Small fix in `automation/agents/mcp/amadeus.yaml` — open a 1-PR ops change. Re-smoke. Verify the audit table shows the failures stop or shift to a different status code.

  (b) **Amadeus test API sandbox flake.** Confirm by reproducing with curl using a known-good payload from Amadeus docs (their example queries). If their own example also returns 500, that's their sandbox. No code change. Document in a sync issue (`sync/issues/2026-05-11-amadeus-test-api-500s.md`) with: characterisation summary, sample curl + response, link to Amadeus status page if any, recommendation (switch to production API for production runs, retry strategy, etc.).

  (c) **Auth or credential quirk.** If the 500 only happens with our token but a fresh token works, may be a token-lifecycle issue. Document and consider adding a token-refresh retry to the MCP playbook (or a stale-token detection heuristic) — that's a follow-up round, not in scope here.

  Pick the smallest correct verdict. Don't ship speculative fixes.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_5_document_findings:
Write a sync issue at `sync/issues/2026-05-11-amadeus-test-api-500s.md` capturing:
  - The characterisation table from phase 1
  - The curl reproduction from phase 2 (sanitise tokens — never echo the full Amadeus client_secret or access_token)
  - The verdict from phase 4
  - Recommendations / follow-up sketch
If verdict (a) lands, also commit the ops PR. If (b) or (c), the round is sync-issue + memory-entry only.

1_characterise_failure_pattern.objective: SQL the audit table to characterise the 500s.
spec: Query travel_agent_events for render_amadeus_failure rows (last 7 days). Group by intent + status_code. Report frequency.
Pull 3-5 full failure payloads via the second query in the design brief.
If the audit table has fewer than 5 failure rows total, run 5-10 fresh travel smokes across all four real intents to populate the dataset, then re-query.
Note any pattern — single endpoint vs all, time-clustered vs uniform, status code distribution (500 only vs 500/502/503 mix).
1_characterise_failure_pattern.verify: Characterisation table written in result file. Pattern named (intent-specific / endpoint-wide / time-clustered).

2_reproduce_with_curl.objective: Reproduce a representative 500 via direct curl against the Amadeus test API.
spec: Fetch a fresh Amadeus access token via the OAuth2 client_credentials flow (use the same client_id/client_secret resolved from the keychain — read-only, never echo).
Build a curl POST to the failing endpoint using the EXACT payload shape our MCP playbook sends (read amadeus.yaml's amadeus_search_<tool> step to see the exact body).
Capture the full response body. If the response includes an `errors[]` array, capture status/code/title/detail per error.
Sanity check: also run curl against a known-good Amadeus example payload from their docs (e.g. their `/v2/shopping/flight-offers` Try-It example). If that also fails, the sandbox is flaky.
2_reproduce_with_curl.verify: 500 reproduced with curl. Response body captured (sanitised). Known-good payload either fails (sandbox flake) or succeeds (our payload has a bug).

3_compare_against_docs.objective: Diff our payload against the current Amadeus self-service spec.
spec: Look up the spec for the most-failing endpoint at developers.amadeus.com.
Diff: required parameters, formats, path/version, headers.
Note any deviation. Note any test-API-specific limitations Amadeus documents (e.g. 'test API only has data for X cities').

4_verdict_and_fix.objective: Pick verdict (a/b/c). If (a), ship a small ops PR.
spec: Choose the smallest correct verdict from the design brief.

(a) Fix on our side: branch `kadyapam/amadeus-mcp-fix-5xx`, open 1-line ops PR with the corrected payload shape. PR title: `fix(amadeus-mcp): correct <field> shape so test API stops returning 500`. Re-smoke after merge.
(b) Sandbox flake confirmed: no PR. Skip to phase 5.
(c) Auth/credential quirk: no PR this round. Document in sync issue, propose a follow-up round for token-refresh-on-401.
4_verdict_and_fix.verify: Verdict named. If (a), PR merged and re-smoke shows the failure stops or changes status.

5_document_findings.objective: Write the sync issue + memory entry.
spec: Write `sync/issues/2026-05-11-amadeus-test-api-500s.md` with: characterisation table, curl reproduction (sanitised), verdict, recommendations.
Sanitise: never include the full Amadeus client_secret or access_token. First 6-10 chars max for verification.
Memory entry under `memory/inbox/2026/05/...md` summarising the round outcome.
5_document_findings.verify: Sync issue + memory entry committed in ai-meta. Result file written.

6_bump_ai_meta_pointers.objective: If verdict (a), bump repos/ops gitlink. Otherwise no submodule changes.
spec: If ops PR landed: `cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta && git submodule update --remote repos/ops && git add repos/ops && git commit -m 'chore(sync): bump ops for amadeus mcp 5xx fix'`. Don't push.
If no PR: skip this phase.
6_bump_ai_meta_pointers.verify: ai-meta has a fresh local commit (only if (a)).

expected_state.description: The Amadeus 500 pattern is characterised in a sync issue. Either a small ops fix lands (verdict a) OR the failure is confirmed as sandbox flake / auth quirk and follow-up is named (verdicts b/c).
expected_state.verify_pattern: Result file's verdict field is one of {a, b, c} and matches a corresponding sync issue + (optional) ops PR.

denied_tools: Modifying repos/noetl, repos/gui, repos/e2e, repos/docs — investigation + (optionally) ops only round
Modifying the Vertex AI or Ollama MCP playbooks
Modifying classify_intent / classify_via_* / log_classification / render_* / merger steps
Modifying the travel runtime (the ops PR, if any, lives in mcp/amadeus.yaml only)
Echoing the full Amadeus client_id, client_secret, or access_token — first 6-10 chars max
Reaching for production Amadeus API endpoints (test API only — switch to production is a future round, not this one)
preferred_tools: psql (or kubectl exec to pgbouncer) for audit-table SQL
curl for direct API reproduction (sanitise tokens)
noetl status <id> --json | jq for execution-level inspection
git apply / git commit -F - / git push (only if verdict a)
gh pr create / gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch (only if verdict a)
result_file_instructions: Write bridge/outbox/20260511-020500-amadeus-500-investigation.result.json. Required fields: {id, from: 'codex', to: 'claude-cowork', completed_at, overall_status, phases: {1_characterise_failure_pattern: {audit_rows_examined: int, intents_failing: [], status_codes_seen: [], failure_pattern_summary}, 2_reproduce_with_curl: {endpoint_reproduced, response_status, response_body_sanitised, known_good_payload_also_failed: bool}, 3_compare_against_docs: {deviations: [], test_api_limitations_relevant: bool}, 4_verdict_and_fix: {verdict: 'a' | 'b' | 'c', pr_url, merged_sha, re_smoke_result_change}, 5_document_findings: {sync_issue_path, memory_entry_path}, 6_bump_ai_meta_pointers: {ops_sha, ai_meta_commit_local}}, regressions_found, iterations, verdict, notes, deferred_followups}. Include the full sync issue content inline if convenient.
